articles.headline: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense With Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity for AI Models
articles.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to supply the U.S. Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions to run AI models, marking a major shift in defense infrastructure and potentially creating a new revenue stream for SpaceX while reducing reliance on traditional cloud providers.

articles.headline: Stocks Sink on Anxiety About Tech and A.I. Spending Amid Rising Chinese Competition
articles.summary: Global markets tumbled as investor unease surged over massive AI spending amid intensified competition from China, with the S&P 500 falling 1% and the Nasdaq down 1.4%. Nvidia, Alphabet, Meta, and SpaceX all saw significant declines, while Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. shares dropped over 7% after announcing a $100 billion U.S. investment.

articles.headline: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft for AI Gadgets
articles.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole over 400 Apple engineers' trade secrets to develop unrevealed AI gadgets, creating the single largest legal risk ahead of OpenAI's confidential $730 billion IPO filing in September[4][6]. This litigation could delay or derail OpenAI's public debut, impacting investor confidence and AI infrastructure investment plans for enterprise partners.
